text: Strings with the same symbol at the front and the back are so 1950s ASCII-think.  PEG, unlike 1950s CFG ideas, can recursively match brackets more easily if the front and back symbols are different (e.g. unicode, at the very least).

text: That's a great point, thanks. I'll raise an issue.
